description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a rectangular array of photovoltaic panels mounted on a robust metallic frame. The panels are arranged in a single, continuous row, each with a dark, grid-like surface typical of monocrystalline or polycrystalline silicon solar cells. The supporting structure beneath the panels consists of angled metal trusses, likely made of aluminum or galvanized steel, providing stability and elevating the array above the ground. The underside of the structure reveals additional components, possibly junction boxes or mounting hardware, but no visible inverter or cabling. The overall design is utilitarian, optimized for maximum sunlight exposure and durability in outdoor urban environments such as parking lots, rooftops, or open public spaces. The color palette is dominated by dark blue-black panels, metallic gray supports, and some lighter accents from structural elements.
description: A rectangular solar panel array mounted on a sturdy metallic frame, featuring dark photovoltaic panels and angled metal supports. Designed for efficient solar energy capture in urban outdoor settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the solar panel array and the supporting truss structure beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the array, the angled supports, and some internal structural details.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the panels, the truss framework, and the overall length of the installation.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the depth and the arrangement of the support structure.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled aluminum or steel truss frame
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 6 panels
